original_abstract: Efficient separation of ethylene (C₂H₄) from ternary mixtures containing acetylene (C₂H₂) and ethane (C₂H₆) remains a critical industrial bottleneck in the production of polymer-grade feedstocks. In this work, we report the synthesis and characterization of a robust metal-organic framework, HIAM-111, designed for the preferential adsorption of light hydrocarbons. Adsorption isotherms and thermodynamic studies reveal that HIAM-111 exhibits high selectivity for C₂H₂ and C₂H₆ over C₂H₄, enabling the direct recovery of high-purity ethylene in a single separation step. Specifically, using a Dual-site Langmuir-Freundlich model to evaluate competitive binding, we analyzed the material's performance under industrially relevant conditions. Breakthrough experiments for a ternary C₂H₂/C₂H₄/C₂H₆ (1/90/9) mixture at 298 K demonstrate that HIAM-111 can produce 99.5% pure ethylene with high productivity (0.0248 L/kg). Furthermore, the adsorbent exhibits excellent structural stability across various solvents and maintains consistent performance over multiple cycling tests. These findings, supported by molecular simulations and experimental analysis, highlight HIAM-111 as a promising candidate for efficient, energy-saving olefin purification.
humanized_abstract: The separation of ethylene (C2H4) from mixtures containing smaller amounts of acetylene (C2H2) and ethane (C2H6) is a major industrial problem in the production of polymer-grade feedstocks. Here, a stable metal–organic framework (MOF), HIAM-111, that favours the adsorption of light hydrocarbons is synthesized and characterized. Through adsorption isotherms and thermodynamic analyses, the highly selective adsorption of C2H2 and C2H6 towards C2H4 is validated, and the direct separation of ethylene with high purity in a single step is confirmed. By implementing a Dual-site Langmuir-Freundlich model in the investigation of competitive adsorption at industrially relevant conditions, the performance of HIAM-111 for a C2H2/C2H4/C2H6 (1/90/9) ternary mixture at 298 K showed a purity of 99.5% ethylene at a high ethylene productivity of 0.0248 L/kg. The stability of the MOF structure towards various solvents, as well as its adsorption performance for multiple cycles are confirmed. Elaborated on the basis of experimental data as well as molecular simulations, the performance of HIAM-111 confirms that it is a high-efficient energy-saving material for olefin purification.

Through adsorption isotherms and thermodynamic analyses, the highly selective adsorption of C2H2 and C2H6 towards C2H4 is validated, and the direct separation of ethylene with high purity in a single step is confirmed. By implementing a Dual-site Langmuir-Freundlich model in the investigation of competitive adsorption at industrially relevant conditions, the performance of HIAM-111 for a C2H2/C2H4/C2H6 (1/90/9) ternary mixture at 298 K showed a purity of 99.5% ethylene at a high ethylene productivity of 0.0248 L/kg. The stability of the MOF structure towards various solvents, as well as its adsorption performance for multiple cycles are confirmed. Elaborated on the basis of experimental data as well as molecular simulations, the performance of HIAM-111 confirms that it is a high-efficient energy-saving material for olefin purification.
